title: hieracium carpathicum subsp. gemellum
intro: Hieracium carpathicum subsp. gemellum, also known as the Carpathian hawkweed or mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and wounds due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hieracium carpathicum subsp. gemellum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
